general_news.title: UK PM Starmer says he plans to remain in office despite crushing local elections defeat
general_news.summary: The appointment comes amid crushing results for Prime Minister Starmer's ruling Labour Party in local elections.

general_news.title: NVIDIA Names Suzanne Nora Johnson to Board of Directors
general_news.summary: NVIDIA today announced that it has named to its board of directors Suzanne Nora Johnson, effective July 13, 2026.

general_news.title: Gundlach Warns About the Risks Facing Private Credit
general_news.summary: Jeffrey Gundlach, CEO and CIO of DoubleLine, discusses the current state of the private credit market. Gundlach compares the present moment in private credit to the conditions seen in 2007, highlighting significant risks and potential domino effects. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

general_news.title: Why Japan and South Korea Won’t Go Nuclear
general_news.summary: America still needs to worry about proliferation in East Asia.

general_news.title: This Is Not the World Russia Wants
general_news.summary: A belligerent America is foiling Putin’s strategy.

general_news.title: India's fiercest female politician faces a fight for survival
general_news.summary: Mamata Banerjee loses West Bengal state, throwing her party - and political future - into doubt.

general_news.title: Abandoned and Ungoverned: Lebanon’s Palestinian and Syrian Populations and the Emerging Radicalization Landscape
general_news.summary: Inside Lebanon, the conditions for the next extremist uprising are quietly taking root.In the summer of 2007, the Lebanese Armed Forces fought Fatah al-Islam for three months inside Nahr al-Bared, a Palestinian refugee camp in northern Lebanon. Fatah al-Islam was a Salafi-jihadist group that exploited the camp’s power vacuum to establish a strong recruitment base, drawing recruits from Palestinian, Syrian, and broader Arab networks. Once the dust settled, more than 400 people were dead, an

general_news.title: The collapse of teen fertility in the digital era
general_news.summary: Teen fertility collapsed globally starting around 2007. This affected countries across the income and policy spectrum. This paper argues that smartphones changed how teens spend time with each other, and that this change in turn drove the collapse in teen fertility. Once enough teens are on the phone, being on the phone is where the […] The post The collapse of teen fertility in the digital era appeared first on Marginal REVOLUTION.

general_news.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
general_news.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

general_news.title: America Should Be Israel’s Partner, Not Its Patron
general_news.summary: The pro-Israel case for ending U.S. aid

general_news.title: Google confirms plans for data center in LaGrange, Georgia
general_news.summary: Company linked to project earlier this month after Fire Department rescued worker from data center roof

general_news.title: Trump’s posting even more AI-generated Trump-Jesus fan art
general_news.summary: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about Big Tech power plays in Washington and beyond. (And when I say beyond, I mean the great beyond, like Heaven, maybe.) If you've found your way to this newsletter from the wild, annual subscriptions are currently 50 percent off. general_news.title: The prophet and the mysterious death of Charmain Speirs
general_news.summary: A BBC Disclosure investigation has uncovered significant questions about what happened at the hotel where Charmain died.

general_news.title: Clippy, Microsoft’s hapless Office assistant, was retired 25 years ago today — its irritating spirit lives on in 100+ Copilots
general_news.summary: Microsoft’s Clippy was put out to pasture a quarter century ago. This hapless, and some would add ‘irritating,’ productivity assistant would no longer be enabled by default in Office, starting April 11, 2001.

general_news.title: What is Strategic Rivalry? Why Should We Care?
general_news.summary: The states most likely to draw America into its next major crisis or war are not unknowns. They are the usual suspects: The same handful of states that have threatened the United States repeatedly across decades. Interstate rivals have caused roughly 80 percent of history’s wars and the odds of any given rivalry ending peacefully are little better than a coin toss. Yet America’s key strategy documents since the 2017 National Security Strategy have used phrases like great power compet

general_news.title: Intel is going all-in on advanced chip packaging
general_news.summary: Intel is hoping to cash in on the AI boom.

general_news.title: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
general_news.summary: Today, I’m talking with Chuck Robbins, CEO of Cisco. Cisco is one of those big companies that everyone has heard of but that most of us don’t have to interact with very much; it’s not really a consumer brand.
